LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT CAUSES OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION THAT ARE CHALLENGED BY THE DEMURRER. AS TO THE BREACH OR CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS NEED TO CLARIFY THAT THE LEASES ARE WRITTEN AND EITHER ATTACH THE LEASES OR PLEAD THEIR MATERIAL TERMS. AS TO THE IMPLIED COVENANT CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT. ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENTLY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joyce Jordan, Esq., Law Office, 415-621-1984, for Plaintiff and opposing party

No other appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANT MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP'S Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the breach of contract and breach of implied covenant causes of action and overruled as to all other causes of action that are challenged by the demurrer. As to the breach or contract cause of action, plaintiffs need to clarify that the leases are written and either attach the leases or plead their material terms. As to the implied covenant cause of action, plaintiffs must plead the existence of a contract. All other causes of action are sufficiently pled. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
